tags: 13C, 15N solid-state MAS NMR chemical shift assignment for YadA membrane anchor domain.
Chemical shift assignment of a membrane protein using a single, uniformly 13C, 15N labelled microcrystalline sample.

saveframes.name: citation_786
tags: Assignment and secondary structure of the YadA membrane protein by solid-state MAS NMR.

saveframes.name: assembly
tags: It is a symmetrical homo-trimer. Each monomer contributes four beta strands and one alpha helix to form a trimeric beta barrel.

data:
Three subunits form a symmetrical beta barrel and transport extracellular domain
of the protein out of the cell. Strand 4 of subunit 1 is in contact with strand
1 of the neighbouring subunit.
YadA belongs to the family of trimeric autotransporter adhesins (TAA). The
anchor domain forms a beta barrel in the outer membranes and exports the
extracellular domain outside the cell.
